one_liner: SPY -0.14% to $745 — market broadly flat despite the Meta-driven chip selloff and the start of Q3 2026; sentiment score 0.879 is highest in the watchlist, reflecting broad bull market participation.

price_action.intraday_story: SPY opened flat, tested $749.43 high, then pulled back to $742.38 before closing near unchanged at $745.76. Quarter-start portfolio rebalancing often creates intraday volatility. The -0.14% close masks significant cross-sector divergence: chip names fell 7-10% while AI software names like APP, PLTR rose 8-10%.

news_catalyst.headline: S&P 500 broadly flat to start Q3 2026 as Meta cloud announcement creates sector rotation from hardware to software AI
news_catalyst.summary: The dominant theme on Q3 opening day is sector rotation: money is leaving AI hardware/infrastructure (memory chips, foundries, GPU-adjacent) and flowing into AI software (enterprise analytics, ad tech, defense AI). This rotation is a maturation signal — the market is moving from 'build the infrastructure' to 'who profits from using the infrastructure.' SPY's -0.14% masks this internal churning.

technical.summary: SPY is consolidating at all-time high levels. Below-average volume is typical for quarter-start sessions. The broad market resilience (only -0.14% despite significant chip sector selloff) demonstrates the strength of the underlying bid.

wyckoff_narrative: SPY is in a macro uptrend with consolidation characteristics. No urgent action needed.

valuation.summary: DCF not applicable to an index ETF.

key_strengths: Jobs market remains firm (jobs report due today)
AI productivity gains providing fundamental earnings support
key_concerns: Sector rotation could cause elevated intraday volatility
fundamentals.summary: Macro backdrop supportive. Q3 earnings season starting in 2-3 weeks will be the next major catalyst.

risks: Jobs report released today — any significant miss creates volatility
Chip sector selloff (-8-10% in key names) could spread to broader market if narrative escalates
